indicators.detail: Registration date for paypa1-verify-secure.com could not be established. Under deny-by-default, an unverifiable domain is treated as unverified, not as clean.

indicators.label: SSL certificate
indicators.detail: paypa1-verify-secure.com could not be reached for TLS verification. Absence of verification is not verification.

indicators.label: IP address in URL
indicators.detail: All links resolve to registered domain names rather than raw IP addresses.

indicators.label: Brand similarity
indicators.detail: paypa1-verify-secure.com contains the brand name "paypal" but is not an official paypal domain.

indicators.label: Subdomain depth
indicators.detail: Subdomain nesting is within normal bounds.

indicators.label: Email authentication (SPF/DKIM/DMARC)
indicators.detail: Sender authentication could not be verified (SPF: fail, DKIM: none, DMARC: fail). The message cannot be proven to originate from the domain it claims.

indicators.label: Reply-to mismatch
indicators.detail: Replies would be sent to recovery-team@mail-secure.ru (mail-secure.ru), not to the apparent sender at paypa1-verify-secure.com. The response destination is concealed.

indicators.label: Credential form in body
indicators.detail: The message body contains an embedded form with credential input fields. Legitimate organisations do not collect passwords inside an email.

indicators.label: URL shortener
indicators.detail: No link-shortening services are used.

patterns.label: Emotional exploitation
patterns.evidence: unauthorized login to your account, which has now been temporarily limited

patterns.label: Urgency manipulation
patterns.evidence: verify your identity within 24 hours or your account will be permanently suspended

patterns.label: Credential harvesting
patterns.evidence: Confirm your password and card details

patterns.label: Action coercion
patterns.evidence: Confirm your password and card details to restore access: [LINK]

2bfa452b7e814f745052f9488f36389bce5effb2.explanation: This message pretends to be PayPal's security team, creating a false sense of alarm about an unauthorized login and a limited account. It imposes a strict 24-hour deadline to pressure the reader into clicking a link and providing their password and card details. This combination of impersonation, manufactured urgency, and a demand for sensitive credentials is a clear sign of fraud.

patterns.label: Urgency manipulation
patterns.evidence: these deals disappear the moment the clock strikes twelve, so act now before your favourites sell out for good

4d8696feb127a89b6b2a31dba1b42115c13c026a.explanation: This is a marketing email for a retail sale, using a deadline and scarcity to encourage the reader to make a purchase. While the language is pushy and creates a sense of urgency, it is legitimate commercial persuasion about a discount, not a threat or a fraudulent request. Therefore, it sits at the top of the promotional band.
